_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how apostrophes work — showing where letters are missing in shortened words, and showing who owns something.

_source.w: We are learning how apostrophes work — showing where letters are missing in shortened words, and showing who owns something.
y: Apostrophes are one of the most commonly used (and misused) punctuation marks in English.
Understanding apostrophes helps us read and write more accurately.
Knowing the two jobs of apostrophes prevents common mistakes in our writing.
eg: We learn that 'don't' means 'do not' — the apostrophe shows where the 'o' is missing.
We use apostrophes to show possession: 'the dog's bone' means the bone belonging to the dog.
We compare singular possession (the girl's book) with plural possession (the girls' books).
We identify apostrophes in texts and explain whether they show contraction or possession.
We edit a text that has missing or incorrectly placed apostrophes.
